page: Microsoft Azure FX48mds is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXMDVS server family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 1008.0 GB of system memory, yielding an allocation of 21.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the server includes 2064 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. There are no onboard GPUs, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21 GB per core, this instance offers high resource density for memory-intensive workloads. It is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and enterprise applications that require massive RAM footprints alongside dedicated compute resources without the need for hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48mds is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXMDVS family, featuring 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture. Managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 1008.0 GB of RAM, which translates to a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per vCPU. Storage needs are met by 2064 GB of local disk capacity, with support for Premium Storage configurations. The server does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for resource-dense environments, the FX48mds is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive memory footprints relative to compute capacity. It is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, and in-memory data processing tasks.
